steps.title: Target the overclaim
steps.content: The student concludes that variations in the IGF-1 gene must account for body-size differences among dog breeds. To undermine that conclusion, evidence should show that IGF-1 is not the only factor affecting dog size.

steps.title: Reject weaker challenges
steps.content: B discusses whether the findings apply to other species, not dog breeds. C supports IGF-1 as important. D says only how common one variant is, without showing whether other factors affect size. The answer is A.
